sourceMovie.title: Traffic
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Traffic (2000) as a thriller theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The thriller and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Layer Cake
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Layer Cake (2004) operates in the same thriller as Traffic, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The dark payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Crisis
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Crisis (2021) belongs to the same conversation as Traffic — both anchor their thriller in drama emotion that doesn't let go. The dark undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Veronica Guerin
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Traffic's thriller stayed with you, Veronica Guerin (2003) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The drama through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Sicario
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Traffic excels in thriller, Sicario (2015) doubles down on crime to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: American Gangster
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: American Gangster (2007) is the natural next step after Traffic: same drama DNA, different perspective. The tense streak and crime elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Blow
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Traffic who respond to its drama will find Blow (2001) immediately familiar. Both films share a crime sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Rambo: Last Blood
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Traffic fans keep landing on Rambo: Last Blood (2019) — the thriller is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: The Marksman
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Marksman draws from the same thriller well as Traffic, channeling drama in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2021 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Blood Father
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Blood Father (2016) captures the thriller appeal of Traffic without simply imitating it. The drama streak running through both films is what puts Blood Father at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Sweet Girl
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The thriller appeal of Traffic — that tense edge, the drama — translates cleanly into Sweet Girl (2021). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

faq.question: What should I watch after Traffic?
faq.answer: Try Layer Cake, Crisis, Veronica Guerin—each shares DNA with Traffic while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Traffic?
faq.answer: Traffic (2000) is best described as Thriller, Drama, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Traffic worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
